input.headline: Why are my health insurance premiums going up so much?

raw.headline: Why are my health insurance premiums going up so much?

winner: I told my insurance company I want to pay more, and they said "Sure, let's put a premium on your premium!

candidates.text: My health insurance company told me my premiums are rising because they discovered I have a "premium" physique, so they’re now charging me a luxury tax for being high-quality.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'premium' with a twist of humor about physique and taxes; concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Because my doctor said I'm basically a walking risk factor now.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist on health insurance risks, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: Because your insurance company just realized you're not a robot — and now they have to actually pay for your healthcare.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on insurance logic with a touch of sarcasm and relevance to the topic."}

candidates.text: My health insurance premiums are going up so much that I'm starting to suspect they're charging me a "premium" for the privilege of being allowed to stay alive.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'premium' with a darkly humorous twist that's both specific and surprising."}

candidates.text: Because your insurance company just found out you're still alive and is charging extra for the privilege.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on a common frustration, concise and specific with a darkly humorous punchline."}

candidates.text: I told my insurance company I want to pay more, and they said "Sure, let's put a premium on your premium!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Playful wordplay with 'premium' and 'put a premium on your premium,' offering surprise and specificity while staying concise and natural."}

candidates.text: Why are my health insurance premiums going up so much? Because apparently, my liver is now a VIP member of the gym.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the connection between a liver being a VIP gym member and rising insurance premiums is weak and not clearly funny. It lacks a strong punchline and feels a bit forced."}

candidates.text: My health insurance company keeps raising my premiums, but I guess they just want to make sure I have enough "coverage" to handle the stress.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'coverage' implying both health benefits and emotional support, but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about insurance premiums. The humor is mild and not particularly surprising."}

candidates.text: My health insurance premiums went down so much, now I have to pay for a doctor's advice—it's just cheaper to ask Google and save my co-pay!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny and uses a relatable comparison (Google vs. doctor), but it doesn't directly address the headline's question about premiums increasing. It also lacks specificity in connecting the premise to the punchline."}
